Elizabeth Willis-Lewis, CSR# 12155, 949-350-4576, elewiscsr@gmail.com, Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER L.P., ABM INDUSTRY GROUPS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Defendants (STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER L.P., ABM INDUSTRY GROUPS, LLC) motion for summary judgment is denied.

The court did not consider defendants' reply evidence. (Moore v. William Jessup Univ. (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 427, 432 n.3 ["Generally, a party moving for summary judgment may not rely on new evidence filed with its reply papers."]; San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, 316 [noting due process issue when considering reply evidence].)

"The papers are to be construed strictly against the moving party and liberally in favor of the opposing party; any doubts regarding the propriety of summary judgment are to be resolved in favor of the opposing party." (Kulesa v. Castleberry (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 103, 112.)

There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Stonestown had actual notice of the dangerous condition. At UMF 6, Mr. Iverson states that he was unaware of any complaints of an oily substance in the hallway. (Iverson Decl., par. 10.) But at deposition, he admitted that Marugame Udon had a history of not properly bagging its garbage, which was transported in that hallway. (Hutlin Decl., Ex. 1 [Iverson Depo., 55:23-57:7].) A reasonable trier of fact could infer that Stonestown was on notice that trash leaks was a recurring problem in the hallway.

ABM fails to maintain initial burden of production as to actual and constructive notice. Stonestown also does not maintain its burden of production as to constructive notice. Both parties rely on the DelaRosa declaration to address these issues. The court sustains plaintiff's objection to this declaration as lacking foundation. DelaRosa executed the declaration in English. At deposition, however, Mr. DelaRosa admitted that he cannot understand English. (Hutlin Decl., Ex. 2 [DelaRosa Depo., 53:25-54:20.) Since the moving papers provide no information regarding a certified translation of the declaration, the court strikes it.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARUGAME UDON USA LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Joinder

Marugame Udon USA LLC's notice of joinder is denied. Marugame merely filed a notice of joinder. It did not file any evidence nor a separate statement of undisputed facts. The filing does not constitute a motion for summary judgment. (See CCP 437c(b)(1)[separate statement requirement]; Village Nurseries, L.P. v. Greenbaum (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 26, 46-47 [joinder to summary judgment motion that lacked a separate statement failed to constitute a motion for summary judgment]; Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627, 636 ["While this form of joinder may be sufficient for some purposes [citations], it is not sufficient for purposes of the summary judgment statute."].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
